intro: Operculina palmeri, also known as the wild cucumber or snake gourd, is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Mexican and Central American folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Operculina palmeri include alkaloids, such as operculine and 14-hydroxylinamarin, as well as flavonoids and sterols, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Operculina palmeri is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a powder or capsule.
